fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the entry, as assigned from the specified number series when the entry was created.

fields.name: G/L Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the G/L number for the entry that was created for this maintenance transaction.

fields.name: FA No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related fixed asset.

fields.name: FA Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date of the related fixed asset transaction, such as a depreciation.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry's posting date.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document type that the entry belongs to.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip:
Shows the date on the document that provided the basis for this maintenance entry.
The program filled in the field in one of the following ways:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the date was copied from the Document Date field on the relevant journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the date was copied from the Document Date field on the purchase header.


You cannot change the document date because the entry has already been posted.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document number on the entry.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the external document number that was entered on the purchase header or journal line.
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the number was copied from the External Document No. field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the number can be copied from the Vendor Invoice No. or the Vendor Cr. Memo No. fields on the purchase header depending on the document type that created the ledger entry.
You cannot change the external document number because the entry has already been posted.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the entry.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the entry.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Automatically includes the number zero for fixed asset entries.
You cannot change the quantity because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: FA No./Budgeted FA No.
fields.tooltip:
Used for budget entries for assets.
This field shows the number of the fixed asset or the budgeted fixed asset related to this entry.
The program copied the number from the Account No. field or the Budgeted FA No. field on the journal line.
You cannot change the number because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: FA Subclass Code
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the fixed asset subclass that the entry is linked to.
The program copied the FA subclass code from the FA Subclass Code field for the fixed asset for which this entry was made.
You cannot change the FA subclass code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: FA Location Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the code for the FA location that the entry is linked to.
The program copied the FA location code from the FA Location Code field for the fixed asset for which this entry was made.
You cannot change the FA location code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: FA Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the fixed asset posting group that was used when the entry was posted.
The program automatically retrieved the code from one of the following fields:
If the entry was posted from the fixed asset journal, the posting group code was copied from the FA Posting Group field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a general journal, the posting group code was copied from the Posting Group field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the posting group code was copied from the Posting Group field on the purchase line.
You cannot change the code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the code for the location that the entry is linked to.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal in the fixed asset application area, the program copied the location code from the Location Code field for the fixed asset for which this entry was made.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code was copied from the Location Code field for the fixed asset for which this document was made.


You cannot change the location code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who posted the entry, to be used, for example, in the change log.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Displays the name of the journal from which the entry was posted.
The program filled in the name from the Journal Batch Name field on the journal line.
You cannot change the name because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source code that specifies where the entry was created.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Transaction No.
fields.tooltip:
Displays the transaction number assigned to all the entries involved in the same transaction. If there are any G/L entries, VAT ledger entries or vendor ledger entries associated with the maintenance ledger entry, they are assigned the same transaction number.
The number is used to connect all entries that are created by the same posting.
You cannot change the number because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of account that a balancing entry is posted to, such as BANK for a cash account.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the general ledger, customer, vendor, or bank account that the balancing entry is posted to, such as a cash account for cash purchases.

fields.name: VAT Amount
fields.tooltip:
Displays the VAT amount that was posted as a result of the entry.
When you post, the VAT amount is calculated according to the VAT conditions specified in the General Posting Setup table.
You cannot change the VAT amount because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Gen. Posting Type
fields.tooltip:
Displays the general posting type that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieved the general posting type from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a general journal line, the general posting type was copied from the Gen. Posting Type field or the Bal. Gen. Posting Type field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the general posting type was filled in if VAT was calculated on the line.


You cannot change the general posting type because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the general business posting group that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieved the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a general journal line, the code was copied from the Gen. Bus. Posting Group field or the Bal. Gen. Bus. Posting Group field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code was copied from the Gen. Bus. Posting Group field on the purchase header.


You cannot change the code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the general product posting group that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieved the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a general journal line, the code was copied from the Gen. Prod. Posting Group field or the Bal. Gen. Prod. Posting Group field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice, or credit memo, the code was copied from the Gen. Prod. Posting Group field on purchase line.


You cannot change the code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: FA Class Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the code for the fixed asset class that the entry is linked to.
The program copied the FA class code from the FA Class Code field for the fixed asset for which this entry was made.
You cannot change the FA class code because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Depreciation Book Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the depreciation book to which the line will be posted if you have selected Fixed Asset in the Type field for this line.

fields.name: FA Exchange Rate
fields.tooltip:
Displays the exchange rate used in the entry.
An exchange rate can be used for fixed assets when duplicating journal lines from one depreciation book to another.
The program copied the exchange rate from the FA Exchange Rate field in the FA Depreciation Book table when the entry was posted. If this field was empty, the program copied the exchange rate from the Default Exchange Rate field in the Depreciation Book table.
You cannot change the exchange rate because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount of the entry in LCY.
The program calculated the amount automatically when the entry was posted using the fixed asset exchange rate.
You cannot change the amount because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Maintenance Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maintenance code that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether this entry has been posted as a corrective entry.
The program copied the code from the Correction field from the purchase header or journal line.
You cannot change the contents of this field because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Index Entry
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that this entry is an index entry.
The program filled in this field with a check mark when you:

Posted an index entry for the maintenance of a fixed asset from a journal.


Ran the Index Fixed Assets batch job with the index maintenance option activated and then posted the journal.


You cannot change the contents of this field because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Automatic Entry
fields.tooltip:
Specifies all entries that have been created indirectly by the posting of a journal line.
You cannot change the contents of this field because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series to which the document number on this entry belongs.
You cannot change the number series because the entry has been posted.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the tax area code that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieves the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Tax Area Code field or the Bal. Tax Area Code field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Tax Area Code field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Automatically copies the value from the Tax Liable or Bal. Tax Liable field on a journal line or the Tax Liable field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the tax group code that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieves the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Tax Group Code field or the Bal. Tax Group Code field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Tax Group Code field on the purchase line.

fields.name: Use Tax
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that the tax amount for the entry is use tax.
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the Use Tax field or the Bal. Use Tax field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice, or credit memo, the code is copied from the Use Tax field on the purchase line.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the VAT business posting group that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieves the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the VAT Bus. Posting Group field or the Bal. VAT Bus. Posting Group field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the VAT Bus. Posting Group field on the purchase line.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Displays the code for the VAT product posting group that applies to the entry.
The program automatically retrieves the code from one of the following fields:

If the entry was posted from a journal line, the code is copied from the VAT Prod. Posting Group field or the Bal. VAT Prod. Posting Group field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from a purchase order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the VAT Prod. Posting Group field on the purchase line.

fields.name: Reversed
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the entry has been part of a reverse transaction (correction) made by the Reverse function.

fields.name: Reversed by Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the correcting entry.

fields.name: Reversed Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the original entry that was undone by the reverse transaction.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 3 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 3, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 4 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 4, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 5 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 5, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 6 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 6, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 7 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 7, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 8 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 8, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.
